content:
We're Hiring at Accenture in India! Role: Compliance & Ethics Counsel Manager – Data Privacy 📍 Location: India
At Accenture, trust and ethics are at the heart of everything we do. As data continues to shape the future of business, we're looking for a Compliance & Ethics Counsel Manager (Data Privacy) to join our Legal & Commercial Services (L&CS) team and help drive privacy excellence across the globe. 🌍
About the Role
You'll work with cross-functional teams and senior leadership to lead privacy risk management, regulatory compliance, and global data protection initiatives — shaping how Accenture navigates an evolving privacy landscape.
What You'll Bring
Experience: 9+ years
🔹 Strong expertise in Data Privacy Law, Data Protection, and Risk Management 
🔹 Hands-on experience with Privacy Reviews, DPIAs, Data Breach Handling, and DSARs 
🔹 Deep knowledge of Global Data Privacy Regulations (beyond India) 
🔹 Proven ability to manage privacy incidents, investigations, and regulatory obligations 
🔹 Experience engaging with senior leadership and global stakeholders 
🔹 Strong program/project management and communication skills
Good to Have
🔸 Exposure to AI, Digital, and Technology domains 
🔸 IAPP certifications (CIPP / CIPM) 
🔸 Knowledge of Privacy by Design and Information Security (ICT) 
🔸 Comfort in a multicultural, global environment
Education
•	Law Degree (Master's / equivalent) – Mandatory
•	IAPP Certification – Preferred
Please Note
•	Direct experience in Data Privacy advisory (incidents, DSARs, privacy reviews) is essential.
•	Contracting profiles are not aligned for this role.
Ready to Make an Impact?
If you're passionate about privacy, ethics, and shaping responsible business — we'd love to hear from you.
👉 Apply here: https://lnkd.in/egfWSX5U or mail your CV to v.b.balaji@accenture.com
#AccentureHiring #DataPrivacy #PrivacyLaw #Compliance #Ethics #DPIA #DataProtection #IAPP #LegalCareers #CareersAtAccenture
comment: One challenge in roles like this is keeping DSAR/DPIA execution consistent across jurisdictions where interpretations of lawful basis and breach thresholds still vary widely in practice.
